title: EXPOSÉ II FINITENESS PROPERTIES OF THE FUNDAMENTAL GROUP

pages.title: EXPOSÉ II FINITENESS PROPERTIES OF THE FUNDAMENTAL GROUP

blocks.html:
EXPOSÉ II
FINITENESS PROPERTIES OF THE FUNDAMENTAL GROUPby Michèle RAYNAUD

Let \(k\) be a separably closed field of characteristic \(p\geq0\), \(X\) a connected scheme of finite type over \(k\) and \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X)\) the largest quotient « prime to \(p\) » of \(\Pi_1(X)\). We show, in this exposé, that if \(X\) is a surface, \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X)\) is topologically of finite presentation, and that the same holds for arbitrary \(X\) if one assumes resolution of singularities.

In the case of a surface, the method of proof, due to J. P. Murre, consists in reducing to the case where one has a fibration over \(\mathbb P^1\) having the properties (i), (ii) and (iii) of the proposition below.

blocks.title: Proposition 2.1.
blocks.html:
Proposition 2.1.

Let \(k\) be an algebraically closed field, \(S\) a projective surface, irreducible and smooth over \(k\). Then one can find a blow-up \[g\colon S'\longrightarrow S,\] where \(S'\) is a regular surface, equipped with a fibration \[f\colon S'\longrightarrow\mathbb P^1,\] such that \(f\) has a section \(\sigma\), and that the following conditions are satisfied:

  1. (i) \(f\) is smooth at the points of \(\sigma(\mathbb P^1)\).
  2. (ii) the fibers of \(f\) are geometrically integral curves having only ordinary singularities.
  3. (iii) there exists a nonempty open set \(U\) of \(\mathbb P^1\) such that the fibers of \(f\) at the points of \(U\) are smooth.

The proposition follows from the existence of a projective embedding \(S\longrightarrow\mathbb P^r\) such that one can find a Lefschetz pencil with respect to this embedding (XVII 2.5). Indeed, let \(D=\{H_t\}_{t\in\mathbb P^1}\) be such a pencil of hyperplanes; recall that we have the following properties (loc. cit. 2.2):

1. a) the axis \(\Delta\) of \(D\) cuts \(S\) transversally; the closed subscheme \(\Delta\cap S\) is therefore reduced and formed of a finite set of closed points of \(S\), \(x_1,\ldots,x_n\).
  2. b) there exists a nonempty open set \(U\) of \(\mathbb P^1\) such that, for every point \(t\in U\), \(H_t\) cuts \(S\) transversally.
  3. c) for every point \(t\in\mathbb P^1-U\), \(H_t\) cuts \(S\) transversally except at one point which is an ordinary quadratic singular point.

Through each point \(x\) of \(S\) not belonging to \(\Delta\) passes a unique hyperplane \(H_t\), and the map which, to \(x\), associates \(t\in\mathbb P^1\), is a rational map \(a\) from \(S\) to \(\mathbb P^1\) with domain of definition \(S-(\Delta\cap S)\).

We consider the blow-up \(g\colon S'\longrightarrow S\) of the closed subscheme \(S\cap\Delta\) of \(S\). The scheme \(S'\) is regular and the fiber \(S'_{x_i}\) above a point \(x_i\) is identified with the projective bundle defined by the tangent space to \(S\) at \(x_i\), hence is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\) (EGA IV 19.4.2, 19.4.4). Moreover the rational map \(f=ag\) is everywhere defined (XVIII 3.1). Finally, one can associate to each point \(x_i\) the section of \(f\) which, to a point \(t\in\mathbb P^1\), assigns the tangent vector at \(x_i\) to \(H_t\cap S\).

Let us verify conditions (i), (ii) and (iii). We note that, for each \(t\in\mathbb P^1\), the curves \(S'_t\) and \(S_t=H_t\cap S\) are isomorphic; the morphism \(S'_t\longrightarrow S_t\) is indeed an isomorphism except perhaps at the points \(x_i\), points where \(S_t\) is regular; since moreover \(S'_t\) is a complete intersection in \(S'\), \(S'_t\) is integral, hence isomorphic to \(S_t\).

Conditions (i) and (ii) are none other than conditions a) and b). Finally \(f\) is smooth at the points of \(\sigma\) because \(f\) is flat and because the fibers \(S'_t\) are geometrically regular at the points \(\sigma(t)\).

blocks.title: Corollary 2.2.
blocks.html:
Corollary 2.2.

The notation being that of 2.1, let \(Y\) be a divisor with normal crossings in \(S\), \(Y'=Y\times_S S'\). Then one can choose \(f\), \(g\), \(U\) such that \(Y'\times_{\mathbb P^1}U\) is a divisor with normal crossings relative to \(U\).

We know that, if there exists a Lefschetz pencil for an embedding \(S\longrightarrow\mathbb P^r\), the Lefschetz pencils form an open subset of the variety of lines of \(\mathbb P^r\) (XVII 3.2.1). The same holds for the set of Lefschetz pencils whose axis does not meet \(Y\) and such that there exists a nonempty open set \(U_1\) of \(\mathbb P^1\) such that the intersection of \(Y\) and \(H_t\) is smooth if \(t\in U_1\). It then suffices to construct \(f\) and \(g\) from a pencil satisfying these two conditions and to replace \(U\) by \(U\cap U_1\).

2.3.0. Let \(G\) be a profinite group. Recall that \(G\) is said to be topologically of finite generation if there exists an integer \(n\) such that \(G\) is isomorphic to a quotient of the free pro-group on \(n\) generators \(F(n)\); let \(K=\operatorname{Ker}(F(n)\longrightarrow G)\); we say that \(G\) is topologically of finite presentation if, moreover, one can find a finite number of elements \(k_1,\ldots,k_r\) of \(K\) such that the smallest closed invariant subgroup of \(K\) containing \(k_1,\ldots,k_r\) is equal to \(K\).

blocks.title: Theorem 2.3.1.
blocks.html:
Theorem 2.3.1.

Let \(k\) be a separably closed field of characteristic \(p\geq0\), \(X\) a connected scheme of finite type over \(k\). We assume that the schemes of finite type and of dimension \(\leq\dim(X)\) over an algebraic closure of \(k\) are strongly desingularizable (SGA 5 I 3.1.5) (a condition satisfied if \(\dim X=2\) by [1] or if \(k=0\) by [2]). Then the fundamental group \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X)\) is topologically of finite presentation.
We may assume \(k\) algebraically closed, by SGA 1 IX 4.10.
1) Reduction to the case where \(X\) is a regular surface.

If \(f\colon X'\longrightarrow X\) is a morphism of finite type, we set \(X''=X'\times_X X'\) and denote by \((X'_a)_{a\in A}\) and \((X''_b)_{b\in B}\) the set of connected components of \(X'\) and \(X''\) respectively. When \(f\) is a morphism of effective descent for the category of étale coverings, the fundamental group \(\Pi_1(X)\) can be computed explicitly

in terms of the fundamental groups \(\Pi_1(X'_a)\) and \(\Pi_1(X''_b)\); it follows (SGA 1 IX 5.2, 5.3) that, if the \(\Pi_1(X'_a)\) are topologically of finite generation, the same holds for \(\Pi_1(X)\); if the \(\Pi_1(X'_a)\) are topologically of finite presentation and the \(\Pi_1(X''_b)\) topologically of finite generation, \(\Pi_1(X)\) is topologically of finite presentation.

Since \(X\) is desingularizable, one can find a regular scheme \(X'\) and a proper birational morphism \(f\colon X'\longrightarrow X\); since \(f\) is a morphism of effective descent for the category of étale coverings, it suffices to prove the theorem under the additional hypothesis that \(X\) is regular. Indeed, we deduce from this that \(\Pi_1(X)\) is topologically of finite generation; knowing that \(\Pi_1(X')\) is topologically of finite presentation and the \(\Pi_1(X''_b)\) topologically of finite generation, we deduce that \(\Pi_1(X)\) is topologically of finite presentation.

Now let \((U_i)_{i\in I}\) be a finite covering of \(X\) by affine open sets and let \(f\colon\coprod U_i\longrightarrow X\) be the canonical morphism. Since \(f\) is a morphism of effective descent for the category of étale coverings, we see that it suffices to prove the theorem for the \(U_i\); we are thus reduced to the case where \(X\) is affine and smooth.

From now on we assume \(X\) affine, smooth over \(k\), and \(\dim X>2\). We can then apply the Lefschetz theorem for quasi-projective schemes ([3] V 7.4); if \(Y\) is a « sufficiently general » hyperplane section of \(X\), we have an isomorphism \[\Pi_1(Y)\xrightarrow{\sim}\Pi_1(X).\] It therefore suffices to prove the theorem for \(Y\), which reduces us to the case where \(\dim X=2\).
2) Case where \(X\) is a surface smooth over \(k\).

By the theorem of strong desingularization of surfaces, one can find a surface \(S\) smooth, integral, projective and an open immersion \(i\colon X\longrightarrow S\) such that the divisor \(Y=S-X\) has normal crossings. We apply Corollary 2.2, whose notation we keep.

Let us show that it suffices to prove that the fundamental group of \(X'=X\times_S S'\) is topologically of finite presentation. Since \(X\) and \(X'\) are regular and since we have \[\operatorname{codim}\left(X-\bigcup_{1\leq i\leq n}\{x_i\},X\right)\geq2,\] every étale covering of \(X'\) induces on \(X-\bigcup_{1\leq i\leq n}\{x_i\}\) an étale covering which extends in a unique way to an étale covering of \(X\) (SGA 2 1.11); this shows that we have an isomorphism \[\Pi_1(X')\xrightarrow{\sim}\Pi_1(X).\]

We consider the cartesian diagram \[\xymatrix@C=4em@R=3em{ X'&X'_U\ar[l]\\ \mathbb P^1\ar[u]^h&U\ar[l]\ar[u]_{h_U} },\] where \(h=f|X'\). We denote by \(L\) the set of prime numbers distinct from \(p\) and by \(\bar\eta\) a geometric point above the generic point \(\eta\) of \(\mathbb P^1\). Let us verify that the hypotheses of SGA 1 XIII 4.8 are satisfied. The morphism \(h\) has a section \(\sigma\) because this is the case for \(f\). Let us prove condition a) of SGA 1 XIII 4.7; the fibers of \(h\) being geometrically integral, the morphism \(h\) is 0-acyclic and locally 0-acyclic (SGA 4 XV 4.1, 1.16); it remains to show that, for every locally constant sheaf of sets \(F\) on \(X'\), \((F,h)\) is cohomologically proper in dimension \(\leq0\). If \(\xi\) is a closed point of \(\mathbb P^1\) and if \(Z\) denotes the integral closure of \(\operatorname{Spec}\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1,\xi}\) in \(\bar\eta\), the scheme \(X'_Z=X'\times_{\mathbb P^1}Z\) satisfies condition \((S_2)\) at the closed points of \(X'\) and is regular at every other point, hence is normal; consequently an étale covering of \(X'_Z\) whose restriction to \(X'_{\bar\eta}\) is connected is necessarily connected. This proves our assertion, whence a). Since \(X'_U\) is the complement in \(S'_U\) of a divisor with normal crossings relative to \(U\), \(h_U\) is locally 1-aspherical for \(L\) (SGA 4 XV 2.1) and, for every finite constant sheaf of \(L\)-groups \(F\) on \(X'_U\), \((F,h_U)\) is cohomologically proper in dimension \(\leq1\) (SGA 1 XIII 2.4), whence condition b). Since \(\mathbb P^1\) is normal and \(T=\mathbb P^1-U\) of codimension 1, we have \[\operatorname{prof}_{\mathrm{\acute et},T}(S)\geq2,\] whence condition c). The condition \[\operatorname{prof}_{\mathrm{hop},x}(X')\geq3\] holds at every closed point of \(X'\) by the purity theorem, whence condition e). Finally, for every point \(t\in\mathbb P^1\), \(h\) is smooth at \(\sigma(t)\), hence is locally 1-aspherical for \(L\) at \(\sigma(t)\), whence condition d).

By SGA 1 XIII 4.7, if \(a\) is a geometric point of \(X'_{\bar\eta}\), we have an exact sequence \[1\longrightarrow\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a)\longrightarrow \Pi'_1(X'_U,a)\longrightarrow\Pi_1(U,a)\longrightarrow1,\] and the choice of the section \(\sigma\) defines a splitting of this exact sequence. Denote by \(K\) the image of \(\Pi_1(U,a)\) in \(\operatorname{Aut}(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a))\); the group of coinvariants under \(K\), \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a)_K\), is the quotient of \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a)\) by the closed invariant subgroup generated by the elements of the form \(x^{-1}qx\), where \(x\in\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a)\), \(q\in K\). By loc. cit. (4.7.4), we have an isomorphism \[\Pi_1^{(p)}(X',a)\xrightarrow{\sim}\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a)_K.\]

Let \(\mathbb P^1-U=\coprod_{1\leq i\leq n}\{t_i\}\) and, for each \(i\), let \(\bar T_i\) be the strict localization of \(\mathbb P^1\) at a geometric point \(\bar t_i\) above \(t_i\) and \(s_i\) the generic point of \(\bar T_i\). An étale covering of \(U\) whose restriction to each \(s_i\) is trivial extends to an étale covering of \(\mathbb P^1\), hence is trivial. For each \(i\), let \(\overline{k(s_i)}\) be the algebraic closure of \(k(s_i)\); the above assertion amounts to saying that \(\Pi_1(U,a)\) is equal to the closed invariant subgroup generated by the images of the Galois groups, \(\operatorname{Gal}(\overline{k(s_i)},k(s_i))\), in \(\Pi_1(U,a)\). By I 5.3, the image \(K_i\) of \(\operatorname{Gal}(\overline{k(s_i)},k(s_i))\) in \(\operatorname{Aut}(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a))\) is finite. Since \(K\) is the smallest closed invariant subgroup containing all the \(K_i\), and since \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a)\) is topologically of finite presentation (SGA 1 XIII 3.2), this proves that \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X'_{\bar\eta},a)_K\), hence also \(\Pi_1^{(p)}(X)\) which is isomorphic to it, is topologically of finite presentation.

Remark 2.3.2. Resolution of singularities is not necessary to prove Theorem 2.3.1 in the case where \(X\) is the open complement of a divisor with normal crossings in a projective scheme, smooth over \(k\).
Bibliography
[1] S. Abhyankar, Local uniformization of algebraic surfaces over ground field of characteristic p = 0, Amer. J. of Math., t. 63, 1956.
[2] H. Hironaka, Resolution of singularities of an algebraic variety over a field of characteristic zero, Ann. of Math., t. 79, 1964.
[3] M. Raynaud, Théorème de Lefschetz en cohomologie des faisceaux cohérents et en cohomologie étale, thesis, to appear.
